briefdescription.para: The maximum bitmap size is 4096x4096 pixels.
Create an empty bitmap with the size (0,0).
Create a bitmap with the given size and all pixels cleared.

parameterdescription.para: The bitmap dimensions. The size is limited to 4096x4096 (cMaximumBitmapSize).

briefdescription.para: Get the bitmap dimensions.
Get a rectangle that represents the bitmap bounds.
Access the raw pixel storage.
Access the raw pixel storage for modification.
Read one pixel.

simplesect.para: The pixel state, or false if pos is outside the bitmap.

briefdescription.para: Read a 2x2 pixel block encoded as four bits.

parameterdescription.para: The quad position in half-resolution coordinates.

simplesect.para: Bit mask with top-left/top-right/bottom-left/bottom-right pixels in bits 0..3.

briefdescription.para: Read the four cardinal pixels as a bit-mask.

detaileddescription.para:
Clockwise bit-order: right, down, left, up
pos
The center pixel position.
The bit mask, where bits 0..3 represent the pixels in clockwise order starting from the right.

briefdescription.para: Read the ring of eight pixels surrounding the given position as a bitmask.

detaileddescription.para:
Clockwise bit-order: 0:E, 1:SE, 2:S, 3:SW, 4:W, 5:NW, 6:N, 7:NE
pos
The center pixel position.
The bit mask, where bits 0..7 represent the pixels in clockwise order starting from the right.

briefdescription.para: Get a rectangle around all set/cleared pixels.

parameterdescription.para: If true, return the bounding box of set pixels, otherwise cleared pixels.

simplesect.para: The bounding rectangle of the specified pixels or an empty rectangle if none are set/cleared.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of set/cleared pixels in this bitmap.
Set one pixel in the bitmap.

simplesect.para: Positions outside the bitmap are ignored.

briefdescription.para: Mirror the bitmap horizontally in-place.
Invert the bitmap in-place.
Get an inverted version of this bitmap.
Convert this bitmap to an outlined version.

detaileddescription.para: The bitmap needs to have a margin large enough for the outline. Algorithm: If at a cleared source pixel is surrounded by at least one set pixel, the target pixel is set.

briefdescription.para: Create a new bitmap expanded with a given margin.

detaileddescription.para:
Negative margins cut sections from the bitmap.
margins
The margins for the expansion.
value
The value to fill the expanded area with.
The expanded/shrunk bitmap. If the new width or height is zero, an empty bitmap is returned.

briefdescription.para: Draw pixels from a numeric bit mask.

parameterdescription.para: The top left corner where to draw the bit mask.

briefdescription.para: Draw another bitmap to the given position.

parameterdescription.para: The top left corner of the bitmap to draw.

briefdescription.para: Fill a rectangle with a given pixel state.

detaileddescription.para:
Positions outside this bitmap are ignored.
rect
The rectangle to fill.
value
The new pixel state.

briefdescription.para: Perform a flood fill from the given position.

detaileddescription.para:
If the pixel at the given position is already set to the given value, nothing happens. If the start position is outside the bitmap, nothing happens.
pos
The start position.
value
The new pixel state.

briefdescription.para: Convert this bitmap to a simple ASCII representation.

detaileddescription.para: This function is meant for debugging and visualization. Use Buffer for a true character matrix. Cleared pixels are represented by dots (.), set pixels by a hash (#).

briefdescription.para: Build a new bitmap using a function.

parameterdescription.para: The size of the new bitmap.
The function to use for building the bitmap. Takes a position and returns a boolean.

briefdescription.para: Create a bitmap from an ASCII pattern.

detaileddescription.para:
Each input string becomes one row. Dots (.) and spaces create cleared pixels, every other character sets a pixel. Shorter rows are padded with cleared pixels to the maximum row width.
rows
The pattern rows to parse.
The created bitmap.

briefdescription.para: A mutable bitmap storing boolean pixels in row-major order.
